In ssh-agent in OpenSSH before 9.6, certain destination constraints can be incompletely applied. When destination constraints are specified during addition of PKCS#11-hosted private keys, these constr

In ssh in OpenSSH before 9.6, OS command injection might occur if a user name or host name has shell metacharacters, and this name is referenced by an expansion token in certain situations. For exampl

OpenSSH through 10.0, when common types of DRAM are used, might allow row hammer attacks (for authentication bypass) because the integer value of authenticated in mm_answer_authpassword does not resis

A security regression (CVE-2006-5051) was discovered in OpenSSH's server (sshd). There is a race condition which can lead sshd to handle some signals in an unsafe manner. An unauthenticated, remote at

A vulnerability was found in OpenSSH when the VerifyHostKeyDNS option is enabled. A machine-in-the-middle attack can be performed by a malicious machine impersonating a legit server. This issue occurs

A flaw was found in the OpenSSH package. For each ping packet the SSH server receives, a pong packet is allocated in a memory buffer and stored in a queue of packages. It is only freed when the server
In sshd in OpenSSH before 10.0, the DisableForwarding directive does not adhere to the documentation stating that it disables X11 and agent forwarding.

ssh in OpenSSH before 10.1 allows control characters in usernames that originate from certain possibly untrusted sources, potentially leading to code execution when a ProxyCommand is used. The untrust
ssh in OpenSSH before 10.1 allows the '\0' character in an ssh:// URI, potentially leading to code execution when a ProxyCommand is used.

cves.description: The SSH transport protocol with certain OpenSSH extensions, found in OpenSSH before 9.6 and other products, allows remote attackers to bypass integrity checks such that some packets are omitted (from

In ssh-agent in OpenSSH before 9.6, certain destination constraints can be incompletely applied. When destination constraints are specified during addition of PKCS#11-hosted private keys, these constr

In ssh in OpenSSH before 9.6, OS command injection might occur if a user name or host name has shell metacharacters, and this name is referenced by an expansion token in certain situations. For exampl

OpenSSH through 10.0, when common types of DRAM are used, might allow row hammer attacks (for authentication bypass) because the integer value of authenticated in mm_answer_authpassword does not resis

A security regression (CVE-2006-5051) was discovered in OpenSSH's server (sshd). There is a race condition which can lead sshd to handle some signals in an unsafe manner. An unauthenticated, remote at

A vulnerability was found in OpenSSH when the VerifyHostKeyDNS option is enabled. A machine-in-the-middle attack can be performed by a malicious machine impersonating a legit server. This issue occurs

A flaw was found in the OpenSSH package. For each ping packet the SSH server receives, a pong packet is allocated in a memory buffer and stored in a queue of packages. It is only freed when the server
In sshd in OpenSSH before 10.0, the DisableForwarding directive does not adhere to the documentation stating that it disables X11 and agent forwarding.

ssh in OpenSSH before 10.1 allows control characters in usernames that originate from certain possibly untrusted sources, potentially leading to code execution when a ProxyCommand is used. The untrust
ssh in OpenSSH before 10.1 allows the '\0' character in an ssh:// URI, potentially leading to code execution when a ProxyCommand is used.

credential_risk.summary: LOW credential risk — no active compromise or significant credential exposure detected.

recommendations: WARNING: 3 checker(s) failed during this scan (exposed_admin, tech_stack, privacy_compliance). The risk score is based on 88% of available data. A re-scan is recommended for a complete assessment.
Implement MTA-STS to force TLS for inbound email and prevent downgrade attacks.
Configure TLS-RPT (add _smtp._tls TXT record) to receive reports about email TLS delivery failures.
Upgrade DMARC policy from 'none' to 'quarantine' or 'reject' to enforce email authentication.
Configure DKIM signing for outbound email and publish the public key in DNS.
Implement a Content Security Policy to mitigate XSS attacks.
Add 'X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff' to prevent MIME sniffing.
Create a security.txt file at /.well-known/security.txt to establish a vulnerability disclosure policy.
Configure your web server to redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS (301 redirect).
Implement a VPN or Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) solution for remote workers.
Immediately investigate and remediate the critically exposed service.
Investigate blacklist listings — likely indicates past spam, malware distribution, or compromise.

data_breach.note: Aggregated C1+C2 costs across all incident types involving data exfiltration

ransomware.note: Combined C4+C5 for backward compatibility; see scenarios_4cat for split

business_interruption.note: Aggregated C3 (revenue loss from downtime) across ALL incident types

b2c.evidence: Sub-industry 'Insurance Agents, Brokers, And Service' is consumer-facing

accountable_institution.evidence: Sub-industry 'Insurance Agents, Brokers, And Service' is a FIC Act accountable institution

regulatory_exposure.note: C2 = POPIA (Section 109; statutory ceiling R10M, 2% turnover used as internal capacity-scaling heuristic for s109(3) factors - not a statutory formula) + GDPR (4% uncapped) + PCI (R1M x non-compliance) + other jurisdictions (R2M each)

catastrophe_stack.note: Statutory maxima per applicable framework, scaled by the enterprise capacity factor for the entity's revenue band. Sanlam-scale (>= R10B) faces the full stack; a micro FSP (< R10M) faces 10% of the stack. Excludes uncapped POPIA Section 99 civil exposure - see Civil Liability Disclosure.

loss_exposure.disclaimer: Figures presented are statistical model output and exclude civil liability (see Civil Liability Disclosure). Selection of cover limit is the responsibility of the insured in consultation with the broker. Phishield does not recommend a specific cover amount.

findings.recommendation: Patch critical CVEs on public-facing servers

findings.finding: High-risk port open: 3306 (MySQL) — Direct database access enables mass data theft; ransomware encryption of data
findings.recommendation: Restrict database access to private networks/VPN

findings.finding: HTTPS not enforced — HTTP does not redirect to HTTPS
No DKIM selectors found across 40 common selector names
findings.recommendation: Enable DKIM signing on your mail server

findings.finding: No VPN/remote access gateway detected — remote access method unknown
findings.recommendation: Implement VPN or Zero Trust Network Access for remote workers

steps.action: Firewall exposed database ports (MongoDB, Redis, PostgreSQL, MySQL, etc.) — restrict to VPN/private network only.
Patch critical vulnerabilities detected via version analysis (OSV.dev) — update affected software packages to latest stable versions.
Patch high-EPSS CVEs (>50% exploitation probability within 30 days) — prioritise by EPSS score for maximum risk reduction.
Upgrade SSL/TLS configuration — enable TLS 1.2+, disable weak ciphers. sslyze analysis shows vulnerable protocols or cipher suites.
Investigate and resolve IP/domain blacklisting — indicates prior compromise, spam, or malware distribution.
Implement security headers: HSTS, Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, Permissions-Policy — prevents XSS, clickjacking, and data leakage.
Deploy a CDN for DDoS resilience and improved availability — single-origin hosting is vulnerable to volumetric attacks.
Configure web server to redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS (301 redirect) — prevents credential interception.
Create a security.txt file at /.well-known/security.txt — establishes a vulnerability disclosure policy for responsible reporting.
Enable DNSSEC to protect against DNS spoofing and cache poisoning attacks.
